claim: Five essential prompt elements for 80/20 results: Model (most important), Purpose (clear goal), Variables (dynamic/static for reusability), Examples (specify exact output), Output (text vs JSON)
actionable: Structure every prompt: choose model → define single purpose → add variables → include examples → specify output type
source_title: MASTER the Prompt: TOP 5 Elements for Reusable Prompts, AI Agents, Agentic Workflows

claim: Dynamic variables (updated per execution) vs static variables (updated during development) enable composability between prompts in chains
actionable: Use dynamic variables for inputs/outputs that change per execution; static variables for rules/constraints that rarely change
source_title: MASTER the Prompt: TOP 5 Elements for Reusable Prompts, AI Agents, Agentic Workflows

claim: Examples in prompts are critical to specify exact output format; without them models produce verbose explanations instead of exact structures
actionable: Always include 2-3 concrete examples of desired output format in prompts, especially when building prompt chains
source_title: MASTER the Prompt: TOP 5 Elements for Reusable Prompts, AI Agents, Agentic Workflows

claim: JSON output enables structured reliable outputs critical for chaining prompts; function calling is essentially JSON output with wrappers
actionable: Use JSON output for all prompts that feed into other prompts; function calling can be fully replaced with JSON output
source_title: MASTER the Prompt: TOP 5 Elements for Reusable Prompts, AI Agents, Agentic Workflows

claim: Prompt composability: output of prompt N becomes variable input of prompt N+1, enabling end-to-end agentic workflows
actionable: Design prompts with JSON output so agent can chain them programmatically: prompt1.output → prompt2.variable
source_title: MASTER the Prompt: TOP 5 Elements for Reusable Prompts, AI Agents, Agentic Workflows
